subject: Elixirs
method: full human/LLM read of transcript 027 (not the heuristic)
note: All 19 tiers below were confirmed to match the stored tierlists.json exactly. The 'why' field is new content extracted from the transcript. Giant Strength elixirs are rated by their drunk-daily use (the broken pattern), which is how the stored S+ tiers were assigned.

Battlemage's Power.why: Locks in three stacks of Arcane Acuity all day (+3 to hit and +3 spell save DC, like +6 casting stat) that can't drop below three; trivially stacks enemies out of ever saving against your crowd control, breaking bounded accuracy.

Hill Giant Strength.why: Sets Strength to 21, so drinking it daily on an 8-STR character gives free 20 Strength from level 1 — effectively two extra feats and a pile of attribute points, an egregious balance error that powers most broken builds.

Cloud Giant Strength.why: Sets Strength to 27 (+3 to attack and damage) and breaks the attribute cap so even dex/finesse builds gain from it; drunk daily it's the same free-stats abuse as Hill Giant, just acquired later.

Vigilance.why: Free Alert feat: +5 initiative (worth ~5x a tabletop point since BG3 rolls initiative on a d4) plus surprise immunity, so you go first in nearly every fight and shut enemies down before they act.

Bloodlust.why: Grants an extra action and 5 temp HP on every kill; even with honour mode denying extra-attack scaling, one bonus attack or full spell per kill is enormous action economy that almost any damage dealer can exploit.

Supreme Arcane Cultivation.why: Free level-4 spell slot daily — strong day-long buffs (summons, upcast Aid for +15 party HP) and extra game-winning casts like 4th-level Spirit Guardians; buyable from any elixir merchant at level 9.

Colossus.why: Enlarge in a bottle: +1d4 damage on every attack plus battlefield-control from the size increase and advantage on Athletics shoves; a generically strong, somewhat overlooked offensive option.

Heroism.why: Permanent stacking Bless (works alongside the actual spell) for +1d4 to attacks and saves all day, plus 10 temp HP; great on dex characters, just edged out of S because most builds prefer Bloodlust.

Viciousness.why: Lowers your crit threshold by 1 and stacks with every other crit source; a minor bonus generally, but builds that care about crits (brutal-critical Barbarian, Smite Paladin, sneak-attack Rogue, GOO warlock fear) get huge value.

Superior Arcane Cultivation.why: Free level-3 spell slot — the level where the best spells live (haste, hypnotic pattern, hunger of Hadar), often granting a whole extra fight per day; better than the lower tiers but still a minor benefit overall.

Guileful Movement.why: Day-long Freedom of Movement: immune to difficult terrain, paralysis and restraint (counters Hold Person/Monster), but lacks the spell's stun-break panic button; solid defensive value that rarely beats an offensive elixir.

Peerless Focus.why: Advantage on concentration saves (a free War Caster feat) plus charm/sleep protection; genuinely useful for support casters maintaining haste, but you'll usually want an offensive elixir instead.

Resistance.why: Halves one chosen damage type and grants immunity to its condition; fight-winning against a damage-themed enemy, but it's purely defensive, demands prior knowledge of the fight, and is often available cheaper from other sources.

Arcane Cultivation.why: Free level-1 spell slot — best used for a free Mage Armor cast or a Paladin Smite, but an extra level-1 spell is never a meaningful boost.

Greater Arcane Cultivation.why: Free level-2 spell slot (good for Aid or an extra Cloud of Daggers); more than twice as good as the level-1 version but still too minor to leave C tier.

Barkskin.why: Sets AC to a minimum of 16 without needing concentration, but doesn't stack with other AC and 16 is low, so only low-AC wildshape Druids benefit — and even they have better elixirs.

Darkvision.why: Day-long 40ft darkvision, which is nearly useless in BG3 since arenas are lit or easily lit and the lighting engine is inconsistent; not harmful so not F, but a waste of clicks — sell it.

See Invisibility.why: Forces nearby invisible enemies to save (using the weak item save DC) or be revealed; a joke effect you can replicate for free with create water, so better sold than used.

Tadpole Elixir.why: Advantage on all attacks after using a tadpole power, but at the cost of disadvantage on Con/Wis/Int saves all rest — crippling the game's most important saves; Heroism or the risky ring give similar upside without the downside.
